entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., a crop commonly grown on sandy soils in warm climates.

entities.name: SANDY SOILS
entities.description: Type of soil where peanut is commonly grown.

entities.name: WARM CLIMATES
entities.description: Environmental climate where peanut is commonly grown.

entities.name: WATER-DEFICIT
entities.description: A condition that can impose a limitation on peanut yield.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the peanut plant.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ratio of mass increase to water transpired.

entities.name: WELL-WATERED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental state used to measure transpiration efficiency in a greenhouse.

entities.name: SOIL DRYING
entities.description: Environmental state used to measure transpiration efficiency outdoors in pots.

entities.name: GAS EXCHANGE PROPERTIES
entities.description: Physiological properties related to gas exchange in plants.

entities.name: FRACTION TRANSPIRABLE SOIL WATER
entities.description: The soil water content at which the decline in transpiration with soil drying is observed.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: Controlled environment used for measuring transpiration efficiency and fraction transpirable soil water.

entities.name: OUTDOORS IN POTS
entities.description: Environment used for measuring transpiration efficiency under soil drying conditions.

entities.name: SECOND-ORDER POLYNOMIAL
entities.description: A mathematical model used to describe the relationship between transpiration efficiency and the threshold for the decline in transpiration.
